steps.title: Find total silicon
steps.content:
The $60.0$ gram sample is $55.0\%$ silicon, so the total silicon mass is:
$$0.55(60.0)=33.0$$

steps.title: Solve for the second piece
steps.content:
Let $m_2$ be the mass of the second piece. Then the first piece has mass $60.0-m_2$. Use the silicon percentages.
$$0.35(60.0-m_2)+0.85m_2=33.0$$
$$21.0+0.50m_2=33.0$$
$$m_2=24.0$$

steps.title: Find silicon in that piece
steps.content:
The second piece is $85.0\%$ silicon, so multiply its mass by $0.85$.
$$0.85(24.0)=20.4$$
The answer is B.
